title: Advanced TypeScript: Leveraging Generics for Reusable Code

questions.sentence: As you transition to advanced TypeScript development Generic's become an indispensable tool for creating flexible and reusable components and functions
Generic's allow you to define a component that can work with a variety of types while still maintaining type safety

For example instead of writing separate functions to handle an array of strings and an array of numbers you can use a generic type parameteroften represented as Tto handle any type provided at runtime
Beyond simple generics mastering Mapped Type's and Conditional Type's allows for highly sophisticated type transformations such as creating a readonly version of an existing interface
Utilizing these advanced features leads to a much more robust developer experience as it provides precise IDE autocompletion and catches potential errors before the code is even run
